name: Blue Otters
description: Larger than common otters, with flattened, sharp-crested tails. Travel in small daytime groups feeding on fish eggs, small aquatic mammals and birds; den in sandbars or riverbanks, bearing only 1-2 young a year. Their large profile and valuable pelts make them endangered; protected by hunting bans across northwestern Endor, notably Gondor.

name: Cavern Wings
description: Cavern Wings are large, intelligent Bats found predominantly in Moria, although they have been reported in other caverns in the Misty Mountains. They seem to have a very sophisticated "biting order," culminating in a leader which directs the other bats in their group hunts. Their weapon is their razor sharp claws, with which they rip their victims into bite-sized shreds. Their voices are low-pitched for bats, allowing most victims some warning of their approach, although this rarely helps as the Cavern Wings are very quick and maneuverable. They have dark gray to black fur, often speckled in younger individuals; the best defense is to slay the leader, after which the rest retreat until the flock chooses a new leader.

name: Sea Eagles
description: Smaller, swifter kinsmen of the fierce Red Eagles, Sea Eagles live along the coasts of the Romenear (Q. "East Sea") and the eastern Haragaer (S. "South Sea"). There, they hunt sea mammals, large fish that swim near the surface, and even sea turtles. The bane of Water Drakes, Sea Eagles are often worshipped as saviors by the seafaring tribes of the remote eastern isles. Sea Eagles have yellowish-gold underplumage and blue-green feathers on the tops of their backs and wings. They have golden beaks.

name: Carnantor
description: Huge ocean-dwelling Eels, the Carnantor (Q. "Red Maws"; sing. "Carnanto") are among the most dangerous of water beasts. They reside in the reefs in the eastern portions of the South Sea (S. "Haragaer") and the southern reaches of the East Sea (Q. "Romenear"), favoring the ample shelter, rich food sources, and warm waters off Endor southeastern coast. These seemingly ever-hungry denizens of the undersea shelf subsist on sharks and other large fish, battling Kraken, Whales, and Fell Turtles for control of the waters. Carnantor are particularly dominant and dangerous in the spring, when the Eels return to their spawning grounds off the coasts of Waw, Cevra, Arth, and Vulm Shryac, crowding the reefs and feeding and mating in an awe-inspiring frenzy. The male is up to 12 feet long, while the female reaches lengths of 45 feet.

name: Leech
description: While lauded for its medicinal qualities in some cultures, the Leech is only considered a nuisance in the delta. These nasty, slug-like creatures fasten to anything warm-blooded and use their serrated tongue to rasp through to a blood vessel. Then they remain fixed to their prey, gorging themselves until they are so full they slip off.

name: Vereut Eagles
description: The Vereut Eagle inhabits the hills of eastern Eriador. It is black over most of its body and wings, but its hue reaches a dark purple near the face and wing tips. The Eagle beak and eyes stand in stark contrast, for they both are a bright yellow color. When hunting, they fly silently, low to the ground, enabling them to skirt the tops of hills and ridges to surprise their favorite prey: Gophers and Rock Hydrax. This method of hunting is startling to those who are unacquainted with the beasts, and many an Eriadoran has suddenly found himself in a brief, bewildering combat at the crest of a hill. Several Dunlanders wear scars on their faces attesting that these chance meetings can be dangerous.

name: Hummerhorns
description: Surely one of Endor's most vicious insects, the rare but often lethal Hummerhorn is the stuff of legend. Each weighs 6-8 lbs and has a razor sharp, pointed proboscis of chitin (transparent horn). It gores a victim to death, then feasts on the corpse with three sets of mandibles. They resemble gigantic black and grey wasps, and their only failing is the noise they make whenever they approach.

name: Black Swans
description: Black Swans are found from Mordor and northward into Wilderland, as far as the southern reaches of Mirkwood. They are also quite common in the river valleys of Nurad and Rhovanion, especially along the River Anduin. They are large for Swans and have been known to mob and overturn boats, usually because the vessels had come too close to the Swans' huge nests.

name: Gelyngyl
description: Gelyngyl (S. "Green Spiders"; sing. "Galungol") are the largest of the so-called "Animal Spiders"—that is, Spiders that are not monsters. Deep green on top and light green on their undersides, they are difficult to discern in a thick forest. Their coloring, coupled with their arboreal nature, makes them very hard (-20) to discern when they are in their natural habitat. Hiding in dense foliage, the rapacious Galungol hangs above animal trails and other areas where its prey might be active, waiting to drop upon unwary victims. Then it injects a powerful (6th lvl) reduction poison into any soft tissue it can find, immobilizing its potential kill before carrying it off to the Spider's web home. Gelyngyl webs are located in clusters set high in the trees. Unlike most of its relatives, Green Spiders are exceptionally social and are prone to attack and feed in groups of ten or more. Acting in unison, they are capable of surrounding and herding their victims into traps. Each Galungol has a pad on each of its four rear legs, and the Spider can rub its legs together to create the sounds with which it communicates.

name: Watchers In The Water
description: Freshwater Kraken that normally reside in the pools of the Underdeeps. One emerged into the Sirannon by the West Gate of Moria, damming it into a gloomy lake and, driven by Sauron's hatred, preying on Dwarves and Orcs before it nearly seized Frodo as the Fellowship entered Moria in T.A. 3019.

name: Wels
description: A species of giant catfish, the Wels is an ugly brute. They are usually up to 4-5' long, but sometimes reach 10'. A larger Wels weighs nearly 700 lbs. It has a rounded body with a flattened head and a wide, horizontal mouth, around which are two long sensory barbels and four short ones. Wels are known to swallow prey whole and will sometimes bash boats to see what falls out.

name: Bitterns
description: A wading and fishing bird found in the deltas of northwestern Endor, the Bittern, or Northern Heron, is known for its strange resonant, mournful cry. It is a slow, cunning, deliberate hunter that feeds on snakes, crustaceans, and fish. Bitterns migrate, spending their winters in the wetlands along the temperate seacoasts and their summers in the lakes and deltas of the North.

name: Sauron's Armies
description: Huge armies of Orcs, Olog-hai (trolls), and Easterling/Haradrim auxiliaries garrisoning the black towers.

name: Cliff Hornets
description: Relatives of the common Mud Dauber Wasp, Cliff Hornets are social insects who build their nest in cracks, crevices, and fissures in the sides of cliffs. Although they will not build nests deep underground, their homes can sometimes be found as much as several hundred feet from a cave entrance if a good nesting site can be found there. These insects are not normally dangerous unless one is an intruder who has blundered into the midst of a nest. Then the outraged Hornets will attack en masse with their sharp, barbless stingers, injecting the victim with a venom that will, at minimum, cause pain and swelling. In addition, many people are allergic to Cliff Hornet venom and may die from its effects. Note: Treat Hornet venom as a first level poison. A normal, nonallergic reaction is -25 to all actions for 1-30 hours. There is a 35% chance (01-35) of allergic reaction, a percentage that may be modified by the victim's Constitution. In the case of an allergic reaction, an incapacitating fever and respiratory distress will afflict the victim for 1-4 days, and five or more stings may cause death in 1 to 10 hours.

name: Black Demons
description: Lesser Maiar allied with Morgoth whose forms and abilities vary infinitely. They can sometimes be persuaded to aid Men or Elves, even in good deeds, but unless the petitioner is carefully protected and binds them with strong constraints little good comes of such bargains; like Balrogs, many partake of natural forces such as fire.

name: Balrogs
description: Evil Spirits of Fire (S. "Demons of Might"), among Morgoth's first allies in the early days of Arda; immortal and singular. The Balrog of Moria (Durin's Bane), perhaps the last of his kind, was roused by deep-delving Dwarves, slew two Dwarf-kings and drove the rest from Moria, and was finally vanquished by Gandalf. Its First Age kin Gothmog and the White Balrog Lungorthin are long gone.

name: Sturgeon
description: The granddaddy of all freshwater fish, the Sturgeon grows up to 18' long in still waters. It has a scaly skin, which is protected by five rows of bony scutes along its sides. Normally very docile, one of these formidable creatures will only attack if its lair is disturbed. Then, it thrashes about angrily, perhaps ramming or upsetting any nearby river craft. Grey and silver, the Sturgeon resides in shallow seas and rivers, feeding on the worms, mollusks, crustaceans, and small fish that live on or near the bottom. They are prized for their meat and their exceedingly tasty eggs.

name: Barrow Owls
description: The Barrow Owl is a small, rare Owl which has taken to living in the open barrows and abandoned gopher tunnels of Eriador (especially in northern Cardolan). Nocturnal and exceedingly specialized, they are virtually blind when confronted with bright light (e.g., in day- or torchlight). This is important, for they startle easily and swarm like Bees when confronted with a potential foe. They carry numerous diseases, including rabies and lycanthresis, to which they are immune. (Normally 2nd-3rd level, the latter disease is a severe psychiatric infliction that affects victims in 110 days and leaves them with the false belief that they are wild animals.)

name: Chamber Birds
description: White, mute, and almost blind, the odd, fungus-eating Chamber Bird is one of the many strange inhabitants of Moria. They seek out the fungus by their remarkable sense of smell (odd indeed for a bird) and note enemies by their inaudibly high cries, in the manner of bats. Their large, gaping beaks have serrated edges and their bluish talons are both sharp and strong. Totally herbivorous (primarily fungivorous), they rarely attack animals unless surprised or starving, though some instinct for meat seems to remain from a predacious ancestor.

name: Green Asps
description: The Green Asp is the only venomous serpent indigenous to Calenardhon (Rohan) and the northern White Mountains. It is a sociable creature (for a snake), making its home in as many as fifty of its fellows in empty Rabbit or Ground Squirrel burrows. On sunny days, the Asp community will come out to sun themselves en masse on the slopes near their subterranean home. Green Asps have dull green skin and are usually fat and torpid, with puffy poison glands below their eyes. They are considered unattractive even by those fond of serpents, and a fetid odor reminiscent of rotting strawberries does nothing to relieve this impression. The (5th lvl) bite is rarely fatal, but the area around the wound becomes necrotic, turning awful shades of violet and yellow-green before sloughing off, usually exposing the bone. In some cases (15% chance, modified by Co bonus) the injection may cause the entire limb to turn gangrenous and fall off; otherwise the victim will be incapacitated for 3-6 days with high fever and hallucinations.

name: Neekerbreekers
description: Noisy, cricketlike insects that are found in marshy areas in the northerly regions of Endor (e.g., Arthedain), Neekerbreekers are little more than a major nuisance. Their name is derived from their noisy, incessant night cry. Those experienced with and accustomed to travel in these regions soon learn to tolerate the sound of Neekerbreekers, but travellers carrying dried grains, flour, or waybread are well advised to keep these supplies tightly wrapped in areas where the hideous "neek-breek, neek-breek!" can be heard, since Neekerbreekers have been known to infest and devour such food. Neekerbreekers are found in particularly foul swamps. Gigantic relatives of the mosquito, these carnivores prefer solid meat. So, while it does not suck blood, a Neekerbreeker will appropriate small chunks of a man's flesh. Any wound inflicted by a Neekerbreeker has a chance of being infected with Yellow Rheum, a 0 level illness.

name: Blue Terrapins
description: Blue Terrapins (Q. "Luinikrúmori"; sing. "Luinikrúmor") are only found in thickets or very dense, overgrown swamp areas. Their native home is Fangorn Forest. Small, their shells average 18" across, but they have a 12" neck. Thus, a Blue Terrapin can strike like a snake, delivering a 2nd level muscle poison (with a Critical Strike) that can paralyze a small mammal (up to 40 lbs). If it bites, it will paralyze the area or limb it penetrates, partially paralyzing one adjacent area. The poison wears off in 3 hours.

name: Cliff Buzzards
description: The Cliff Buzzard is a large, black-feathered bird of the mountains. It builds its nest on inaccessible cliffs. Often several pairs will nest in the same area, behavior more typical of the Corvidae (crowlike birds) than of the Raptors (Hawks and so on). Cliff Buzzards exhibit a great deal of intelligence (for birds) and will cooperate in hunting. They will band together to mob some large creature and drive it over a cliff so that they may all feast together on the shattered body. Any wounded creature, no matter what its size, may be subject to the unwanted attentions of the Cliff Buzzards. They are most common on the southern slopes of the White Mountains.

name: Ninfiara Wild Aurochs
description: Great wild oxen of the Ninfiara plains — massive, horned and formidable, untamed forebears of cattle. A charging bull is a peril to any hunter, and even wolves think twice before troubling a herd.

name: Orao
description: The Desert Orao is the principal native Eagle of Far Harad. It is quite similar to the Eagles of the North, except that the environment has forced him to play the role of scavenger as well as hunter. These soaring hunters swoop to attack smaller birds or prey on the ground. Unlike the northern birds, the desert Eagle will as readily eat carrion as fresh meat, an adaptation made necessary by the environment. They are aggressive enough to strike travellers along Harad's southern caravan roads. Aside from Men, the Orao are the dominant hunters on the vast plains of southwestern Harad. Their eyries are set in the caverns of the northern Yellow Mountains, and in wind-hollowed caves the highest of the hilly Raj region to the north. In size, they are no match for the Great Eagles, but they are large enough to carry off a shepherd's goat or lamb.

name: Nethairin
description: The Plains Snake (Si. "Nathair"; pl. "Nethairin") is roughly six feet long. It has a thick triangular head and a cream-colored body overlaid with a dark diamond pattern. Hiding in shallow burrows and hunting amidst the tall grass of the steppes or plains, the serpent is a quiet creature that relies on stealth and patience to defeat its foes. Rodents, birds, and small lizards make up its diet. Although ostensibly cunning, the Nathair angers easily and will vigorously defend itself rather than flee, even in a fruitless confrontation. Its venom is rather toxic (level 6); symptoms include swelling, weak pulse, shock, and wide discolorations.

name: Nethairin Erdyr
description: The Nethairin Erdyr (sing. "Nathair Ardor") live in burrows beneath rocks or within caverns and favor highland locales or the cool wastelands of the North. While very similar in coloration to the Nathair of the Plains, the Nathair Ardor attains a length of only 2-3 feet. It angers easily and will defend itself viciously. Its poison is similar to that of the common Nathair, however it is weaker (5th lvl) and takes longer (6 vs 2 minutes) to affect the victim.

name: Marsh Crocodiles
description: Swift but usually inactive, the Marsh, or "Fish-eating," Crocodiles are common in the wetter, warmer parts of Endor. They are inoffensive when let alone, but can be quite deadly when surprised (or hungry). Black, with green stripes and light blue undersides, they are colorful carnivores. Their diet consists of large mammals and birds who enter or come to the edge of the water. After grabbing its prey in its long, powerful snout, the Marsh Crocodile drowns the victim and then tears the flaccid body apart with twisting bites. It spends its nights in the water and basks on shore during the day, feeding on average once every two days, and swallows rocks for ballast.

name: Nethraich
description: The Nathrach (pl. "Nethraich") is an aquatic snake that resides in relatively placid or stagnant pools, marshes, and lakes. Brown bands surround its rust-colored body, which typically measures three to five feet in length. Contrary to popular belief, this serpent does not normally strike in water; most victims are on dry land or a marsh island when they stray into a snake run. These snakes only attack when their "runs" are encountered, and then it bites with astounding quickness. A Nathrach's critical strike is accompanied by a muscle poison that causes bleeding, bruises, and swelling (double all these effects on this and subsequent criticals). The venom is not very toxic (level 2) but is designed to stun small rodents. The Arnathrach (pl. "Ernaithrach"), or Royal Nathrach, is a much larger cousin that inhabits the brackish waters near the coasts.

name: Otrovativi
description: The Otravati (Ap. "Poison"; pl. "Otrovativi") is a small snake found in western Harad. Rarely exceeding two feet in length, it has a murky grey coloring and is easy to overlook. It is, however, not an animal to take lightly. The Otravati's narrow head opens to reveal a surprisingly large mouth full of tiny needle-like teeth. Each tooth is an injector that delivers a dose of the snake's poison on any successful bite (whenever a critical is scored). Locals consider amputation to be the only effective remedy when confronted by the snake's potent (25th lvl) venom, a nerve poison that attacks through the bloodstream and the nervous system simultaneously. The venom is lethal within one hour; only immediate removal of the bitten member will serve as a cure. It is no wonder that the snake bears a name which translates as "poison."

name: Pied Swans
description: Handsome aquatic birds, Pied Swans may be the most intelligent of herbivorous avians. They seem able to remember those who have harmed or helped them (at least for periods of several weeks), and there have been reports of pied Swans leading fog-lost travellers to high ground in marshes. Various rural and primitive peoples, such as the Marshmen of the Nindalf, revere these birds as being sacred to the Vala Yavanna. They are creamy white in color, liberally splotched and speckled with black and gray. Unfortunately, their flesh is tasty and their feathers soft, so that those with more greed than reverence hunt them. Pied Swans are most commonly found in the Nindalf and along the shores of the Anduin in the relatively unspoiled areas of Ithilien.

name: Mordor Cattle
description: Hardy, ill-tempered cattle bred in the grey fields of Nurn to feed the armies of Mordor. Tended by slaves under the lash, they are lean, half-wild beasts suited to the harsh land that raises them.

name: Wargs
description: Great evil wolves of the North, larger and more cunning than common wolves and bound in malice to the Orcs, whom they often bear into battle. They hunt in savage packs, share a crude speech of their own, and gather at the Enemy's call.

name: Kingfishers
description: The smallest of Endor's fisherfowl, the Kingfisher is also the most attractive. It has a large, silvery bill and silver, blue, orange and white feathers. The Kingfisher's riverbank home belies its beauty, though, for it is little more than a dirty hole full of old fish bones and rotting mess. Besides fish, the Kingfisher preys on insects, centipedes, and small lizards and amphibians.

name: Short-Eared Owls
description: A rare day-hunting Owl, the Short-eared Owl is quite small. Like many other Endorian birds, however, it is an intelligent and perceptive creature. They were taught by the Elves to speak and have their own language. One who is close to Nature can derive helpful information from them.

name: Green-Winged Crows
description: A lively, intelligent bird of the marshes and shores, the Green-winged Crow can be tamed even as an adult. They make effective messengers, able to find their way home from any direction within a 75-100 mile radius. However, like most of the Corvidae, they are attracted and distracted by shiny objects. With their shiny black plumage marked distinctively on the "shoulder" of the wing with a patch of iridescent green, these crows make attractive pets.

name: Trusavi
description: An arboreal creature, the Trusa (P. "Leaf Frog"; pl. "Trusavi") is adapted for gliding from tree to tree. It has a broad head, a long slim body, and elongated limbs with webbed feet. Flaps of skin fringe the forelimbs and heels, permitting the creature to extend its body and create a bizarre but effective set of "wings." This bright red and blue frog grows no larger than about 3 inches in length, but it is among the deadliest animals in the humid woodlands of southeastern Middle-earth. If touched, the spines on the back of the Trusa will inject a strong (lvl 4) poison which acts in 1-10 rds. A victim who fails his RR by 21 or more dies, while one who fails by 11-20 becomes blind and falls into a coma; a RR failure of 01-10 leaves the victim blind in 1-2 eyes.

name: Cunara
description: The most common rodent in Cardolan, the Cunaura (Du. "Gophers") of Eriador resemble big stout Rats and live mainly on grass roots. On the plains they are a major nuisance because they live in large colonies of interlocking burrows. Their holes rise up about a foot from ground level but their tunnels run very close to the surface and cannot be easily detected by Sheep, Horses, or Men (leading to many broken legs).

name: Zurkuvi
description: The Zurku (Ap. "Trail lizard"; pl. "Zurkuvi") is found throughout Harad, particularly in the south. It is related to the Gorbet of northwestern Endor (Gorbets are especially well known in Dunland). A dull yellow color with black and red stripes on its head and neck, the Zurku is a colorful creature; it has a bright orange, arrow-shaped patch on its back. It typically grows to a length of 12" and can be found in the summer months. The lizard has no natural enemies because it tastes terrible and secretes a mild nerve toxin from its skin; the poison is absorbed through the skin and paralyzes victims for 10-20 minutes (it numbs the limbs of larger attackers for only awhile, but for most animals is an effective deterrent). What is notable about this relatively harmless lizard is its reputation as a pathfinder: it is believed by most Haradrim that if they are lost and follow a Zurku, it will lead them to a trail or road. This legend is shared by the Dunlendings and appears to have originated among the ancient Southrons during the early Second Age. There may be some truth to this belief, since the Zurku apparently understands that where there are trails there are beasts (and beast-droppings), and where there are beasts there are flies.

name: Aurych
description: The Auroch (pl. "Aurych") is a domesticated breed of the wild cattle or Kine that roam Rhovanion (Wilderland). They vary in color, but most have medium length, dark brown fur on their bodies, and shorter, whiter fur on their legs and foreheads. Males have larger, curving, conical horns. Like all Kine, Aurych migrate with the seasons and tend to wander wide areas whenever breeding.

name: Caru
description: The Caru (Du. "Elk") is a large brown Deer commonly found in northern Eriador, but ranging throughout the cooler parts of northwestern Middle-earth. It is the only Deer species, save the Losrandir, in which both the males and females sport antlers. Caru travel in huge herds of one to twenty thousand and range over vast areas of open country or broken woodlands. Feeding on grass and other tundra plants in the summer, some roam well into the Far North.

name: Zamkavi
description: The Zamka (pl. "Zamkavi") is common to the dry hills of western Harad. Its name translates as "snare," and is derived from this snake's method of attack. The Zamka whips its tail out like a lash and coils about a limb or appendage. The snake's long (7') body is lined with small barbs along each side. The barbs are coated with a poisonous secretion which enters through the skin of its victim. This (7th lvl) muscle poison essentially paralyzes the prey (duration = 20 minutes per ten points of RR failure). When its venom takes effect—in forty-five seconds—the serpent will move to constrict its prey. Once the victim is dead, the Zamka remains with the corpse until time and weather has softened it, then begins its feast. Other animals steer well clear of any kill it guards. Note: the Entangle Table is used with any critical indicating a poisoning; it aims at bare skin, will attack while the victim is moving, and continues until movement ceases.

name: Mulkánar
description: Largest member of the mussel family, the Mulkana is a giant, hard-shelled bivalve with a black shell that protects its soft, white body. This molusk is related to the Giant Clam, although it is larger, quicker, and has a long, vaguely oval, shell. It secretes a foul, acidic, ink (3rd lvl) which stuns larger prey, kills smaller victims, and slowly eats away skin. Found off the eastern shores of Endor, Mulkanar are hunted for their huge (and reputedly enchanted) pearls. At least one of these precious jewels, the priceless Womaw Moon, exceeds six inches in diameter. Legend proclaims that the larger of these pearls serve as Seeing-stones, although none is as powerful as a Numenorean Palantir.

name: Nímaeargyrth
description: Elves call the deadliest of Endor's Sharks Nímaeargurth: "White Seadeath" (pl. "Nímaeargyrth"). Typically large (15+ feet) and always hungry, these unsleeping killers hunt virtually anything that has flesh that they can tear apart and eat. Even wounded Whales have felt the wrath of these white (underparts) and grey (overparts) denizens of the sea. The Nímaeargurth's long, pointed snout is equipped with nine rows of flat, triangular teeth. Those of the outermost row are six to eight inches long. When broken off, the teeth are replaced by those of the next row, enabling the Shark to replenish its saw-like weaponry. Like all Sharks, the Nímaeargurth is a cartilage-skeletoned fish. It bears live young, usually in litters of 7-9 Sharklings.

name: Ûlcercain
description: Úlceracin (sing. "Úlcarcan") are large, red, centipede-like creatures that move in hordes across the dense forests of southeastern Endor. Nocturnal creatures, they feed on rotting vegetation, insects, carrion, and (occasionally) live prey. They are extremely quick and are capable of scaling trees and swimming across placid water. Like ants, they operate in rigidly-defined social groups, if necessary swarming to defend their lairs. Úlcercain generally reside in rotting logs. Most of the so-called "Evil Fangs" are harmless to the Children of Ilúvatar; however, the "warriors" that comprise about twenty percent of any colony will attack virtually anything and anyone. Immobilized or sleeping folk need be careful around these creatures. Armed with powerful 2-3 inch mandibles and a mild (lvl 0) nerve venom, a group of Warrior Úlcercain can slay and clean a Man in a matter of minutes.

name: Egils Vipers
description: A huge, poisonous, predatory serpent, Egil's Viper is one of the strangest hazards of the plain of Talath Harroch, south of Mirkwood. It is said that these remarkable snakes were driven out of other areas of Rhovanion into the Talath Harroch, but this seems unlikely at best. They prey upon eggs, rodents, young animals, and (in the case of the largest Vipers) Men and Kine. Of a yellowish-brown color that blends well with the soil and grasses of the plain, Egil's Viper hunts in the late afternoon or early evening. It may spit its venom into a victim's eyes from a distance of up to thirty feet, or it may bite and inject venom in the normal manner. The Viper's fangs reach up to three inches in length and can penetrate leather and, in some cases, even chain armor.

name: Stetanavi
description: In Apysaic, Stetan means "mischievous." It applies perfectly to this otter-like creature. The beast's body is as large as a big dog, and it is as friendly and playful as the most lively of puppies. It has very short legs, though, so no one would mistake it for a hound. The Stetan's name was earned from the Haradrim who journeyed into the wood, worked a long day gathering herbs, then saw their sacks and parcels playfully torn apart just before they returned home. Only their overpowering air of pure innocence keeps Stetanavi from being killed on sight by most men who glean their living from the wood. As it is, they are usually viewed as a sign of long-term good luck even if they are a short-term nuisance. There have been attempts to tame the friendly beasts, but Stetanavi taken into captivity invariably die shortly thereafter, an occurrence usually blamed on loneliness for their own kind. It is possible to overcome this through the use of a Familiar spell, though having such a puckish creature as a familiar would certainly have its drawbacks.

name: Goral
description: Goral are a wild, golden bighorn Sheep found exclusively in the Downs of Eriador. They have long, curving, whitish horns. Solitary and intractable, they are unique among Sheep. Goral only gather during mating season, when small groups of males join and then seek out the females. Ranked by the size of their horns, the males instinctively know their place, so there is little fighting during these brief unions. After mating, the females travel as a herd with the young for a couple of months and then scatter. Goral have been very profitably interbred with domestic Sheep, producing excellent varieties of wool-bearing species (Q. "Toa" = "Wool").

name: Andamundar
description: The Andamunda (Q. "Elephant"; pl. "Andamundar"; lit. "Long-snouted") is the smaller and more numerous cousin of the famous Mûmak. Like all its cousins, it has thick, pillarlike legs and flat, padded feet; loose skin and large ears provide heat regulation, enabling it to adapt to a variety of ecosystems. Its most notable feature is its trunk: an elongated nose and upper lip that serves as both a sensory organ and a prehensile grasping tool, used to smell, gather food, drink, and as a weapon in combat. Its pair of long tusks serve primarily as digging tools (it subsists on roots, shoots, twigs, fruit, leaves), a typical Andamunda eating about 400 pounds of food a day. Always active except for rest periods at night and a midday break, it roams in a constant search for security and food; its plodding belies its strength as a swift, agile runner that can run down and gore a foe. Unlike the Mûmak, it has a slightly humped back and one (vs. two or three) fingerlike extension at the base of its trunk, plus a gland to secrete scented waste. Like Mûmakil, females travel in herds led by an elder matriarch, bearing one offspring (after 21 months gestation) and raising the calf for 24-30 months. Domesticated by various cultures, used less than Mûmakil as riding animals but much easier to tame.

name: Slow-Fangs
description: The Slow-fang is one of the most dangerous and dominant creatures in the rain forests of southern Endor. Large (9' long) and exceptionally strong, it lives in trees and hunts large mammals and smaller reptiles. Its body coloration varies with the surface it happens to cross, although it is usually brownish and has mottled green stripes. They have friction pads on their feet and specialized scales that enable them to stick to vertical surfaces for indefinite periods, making them naturally agile tree-climbers; this impairs their movement on the jungle floor, where they are awkward runners. Endowed with a strong, 6' long prehensile tongue that acts like a whip or bola, they can strike a foe quickly. Then they bite the immobilized victim with a pair of 7" fangs, injecting a mild (2nd lvl) muscle poison; this disables the prey long enough for the Slow-fang to slowly discard any inedible trappings (e.g., clothing) before swallowing the meal in one long gulp.

name: Wild Goats
description: Essentially descendants of various escaped domestic varieties, the Wild Goats have dirty, shaggy coats and short, black horns. They range all over Middle-earth. Their small, sharp hooves make them agile climbers, and they can swim remarkably well; however, they restrict the beasts' movements in the boglands, where wide feet provide stability in the muck.

name: Umakavi
description: A small tree frog from the rainforests of the Mûmakan in the south of Middle-earth, the Umak (pl. "Umakavi") is notable for the potent contact poison exuded by its colorful skin. Bright lime green and streaked with yellow and orange, the Umak Tree Frog is noticeable, but it is so rare that it is still nearly impossible to spot. The bright coloring warns away animal and bird predators who would be poisoned upon grabbing the frog in their beaks or mouths, but merely makes things easier for clever human or Elf hunters who come armed with nets on long handles. The poison is tenth level in contact with skin; unlucky individuals are killed instantly (upon failing a RR), while the more fortunate are plunged into a coma until awakened by some form of antidote.

name: Astabanhéli
description: The Astabanhe or Giant Pronghorn is an antelope-like creature found mainly in the Pinnath Ceren (S. "Red Fells") and the Girithlin highlands of Eriador. They stand about 4' tall and are dull yellow in color. While renowned for their loud white rump, their short black horns give the Astabanhéli their name. Pronghorns travel in small herds (10-100) and are inquisitive, but they are quick to flee. Movements of their white tail fur signal danger when there is danger or when they have taken to flight. They can travel at speeds of up to forty miles per hour and can swim quite well.

name: Death Shrews
description: Tiny, mild-looking shrews with soft white fur and blue-grey bellies that carry the dread Blue Hand disease: victims turn bluish, break out in boils, bleed from nose and ears, and many are crippled or kill themselves. The shrews are merely immune carriers. Superb climbers that avoid most encounters, but when cornered they bite exposed skin viciously and secrete a foul spray; their spines can bear a Man's weight.

name: Cave Bears
description: Huge bears (Q. "Meglirondor") weighing up to 1,400 lbs, with rough black fur striped brown on paws, ears and face. Paws can snap a neck and claws rend light metal or leather like linen, yet they are retiring creatures preferring roots, berries and fish. Monogamous and family-minded; males travel alone while females keep their cubs until grown.

name: Dire Wolves
description: Large cousin (S. "Gothraug") of the Timber Wolf, found all over northern Middle-earth in family groups (2-11) or packs (10-60). Intelligent, social and mated for life, settling disputes without bloodshed and cooperating in the hunt; unlike common wolves they have little fear of the Free Peoples and will hunt Men, Elves or Dwarves. Up to 150 lbs, 30 mph, leaping 6 ft, with tremendous nightvision.

name: Dappled Deer
description: Akin to the Roe Deer (Fiara) of Eriador, the Dappled Deer is small, hardy, and pugnacious. Only the stags fight, but they are exceptionally skiddish and aggressive, especially in Spring. They usually employ their tough horns, which can effectively penetrate mail. Nocturnal creatures, Dappled Deer browse for food at night. They feed on shrubs and broad-leafed trees. During breeding season, males mark their territory by scratching the bark off trees. Note: The Dúnadan Rangers have frequently used this trait to their advantage by disguising their own trail markings as Deer scratches; only the Rangers understand the subtle differences.

name: Skeletons
description: Skeletons are similar to Ghouls, except that they have been picked clean of all flesh. Somehow their bleached bones hang together, animated by evil forces. While not as inherently disgusting as Ghouls, the sight of an animated Skeleton bearing weapons is still terrifying. The common Skeleton attacks alter the manner of a Ghoul, rushing headlong at its foe and using i t s teeth and claws. Skeleton Lords often bear the weapons that were buried w i t h them and can utilize simple magical items. Having no organs or blood. Skeletons are not affected by bleeding criticals, and all Puncture criticals become Crushes.

name: Losrandír
description: The Losrandir is a hardy, gregarious Deer with a long, low profile and large antlers. They come in many colors—everything from white to jet black—but most are grey or silver. Unlike those of other Deer, the low forward tine of its antlers is branched. Both males and females bear antlers, although those of the female are usually smaller. Female Losrandír (aka "Lesrandír") move in large herds while males travel in small groups or, in some cases, alone. Wolves and Bears prey on weaker individuals, but the Losrandir's greatest natural enemy is undoubtedly Man. The Lossoth of northwestern Middle-earth and the Urd of the northeast hunt the graceful creature for its fur, meat, and utility as a beast of burden.

name: Cats
description: A handsome, long-legged hunter of the plains of Calenardhon, the Grass Cat is now quite rare. Men have killed or domesticated most of its prey and actively hunt the Cats for fear of losing Sheep, Goats, or Kine. A Grass Cat has a thick, short, buff-colored fur that easily absorbs grass pollen in the summer months, giving it an eerie and unnatural yellow-green coloration. The Cat hunts by leaping upon the backs of its prey, digging its long claws into the victim's throat and neck. Even if its victim is not killed by the severing of some vital vein or nerve, it will run itself into a stupor or even to death. There have been cases of Grass Cats killing Men in this same manner. When in heat, courting, or fighting. Grass Cats give off highpitched, raucous shrieks that are terrifying to hear, especially during the long winter nights.

name: Cliff Lions
description: Snowy-white lion (Q. "Rálimba") of southwestern Middle-earth, its core territory around the Bay of Drel and the lower reaches of the Yellow Mountains near Lake Aeluin. Solitary and shy of virtually sheer terrain; active morning and evening, hunting goats, sheep, rodents and carrion, striking swiftly at the nape of its prey.

name: Beaded Lizards
description: Stout, venomous lizards of the dry southern scrub, marked with bead-like scales. Slow but ill-tempered, they deliver a painful, lingering bite when handled or trodden on.

name: Giant Martens
description: Huge relative of the common weasel (S. "Carlûmon", pl. "Cerlymyn"), a fierce, irrational and hyperactive creature that must eat constantly. Totally carnivorous (foraging nuts and berries only when starving), it kills anything it can disable, even Small Bears and Wild Boars. Acrobatic and an excellent climber, it dens in hollow trees and has a keen nose and fair vision.

name: Fishing Cats
description: Aquatic cats most common in the marshes along the eastern shores of the Sea of Rhûn and in the Wetwang (Nindalf) in Ithilien. With no dislike of water and semi-webbed paws, they swim well and flip fish from the water, supplementing their diet with birds, shellfish and amphibians. Mottled grey-brown fur hides them among reeds; kittens can be tamed but stay one-person pets.

name: Nimfiara
description: Larger and rarer than Elk, the Nimfiara (S./Du. "White Hart") is the strongest and most imposing breed of Deer found in northwestern Middle-earth. Reputedly mystical creatures, they range in the ancient woodlands of Rhovanion, Gondor, Calenardhon, and Eriador, and are found deep within the Old Forest and the Dark Wood, and a few have been spotted in Fangorn. The Nimfiara's lustrous silver pelt is prized for fine garments, so it is hunted and trapped by woodsmen. Worse, its horns are said to produce a potion which retards aging. Thus, the beast's value threatens its survival and, aside from the inaccessibility of their habitat, the Nimfiara has few defenses against extinction. They are, however, very wily, and they will put up a good fight when cornered.

name: Blue Bears
description: Monstrous, bluish-grey bear (Q. "Megliluine") found only on the southern slopes of the White Mountains, especially Lamedon (the Morthond Valley) in Gondor. Has a constant innate Presence ability, sensing living beings up to 50 ft. Often attacks without provocation; usually solitary but will hunt in family groups, defending territory even against Men and Elves.

name: Chetmíg
description: Huge, rare grey cat (pl. "Chetmíg") with faint tabby markings for camouflage. A nocturnal predator with massive 5-7 inch forequarter fangs; its body tapers to strong, compact hindquarters and it uses its long tail to balance prodigious leaps (up to 150 ft horizontally, 40 ft vertically). Found in the northern hills and mountains.

name: Merisc Tyka
description: Wild dog (pl. Merisc Tyka) found throughout Rhovanion, Rhun and the lands of the Kykurian Kyn, related to the feral dogs of the Talath Harroch south of Mirkwood. Frequenting fens and moorlands in small packs, it fishes, scavenges carrion and attacks small mammals, and will prey on weak, stranded or sickly Men. It is afraid of fire and easily driven off with torchlight; its dappled yellowish-tan and brownish-grey coat grants a +40 Hiding bonus.

name: Sheep Hounds
description: Popular among shepherds throughout Middle-earth, the favourite being the large, short-muzzled, long-haired Sheep Hound (Q. Huoton). It endures harsh weather and performs even when fatigued, and its size, stealth and speed ward off predators; very loyal, intelligent and fierce, a collared hound is a match for two or three Wolves. The Dunedain also use them for hunting and as guard dogs, though war-dog experiments failed; a smaller breed is kept as a pet for women and children.

name: Madratines
description: Small, cat-like fox of the highlands of Eriador, its white mask and ears standing out sharply against reddish-brown fur. Very shy and nocturnal, it preys on rodents, birds, insects and lizards. Pairs mate for life and generally keep to a single burrow over the course of a year.

name: Red Foxes Of Calenardhon
description: Unlike the common variety, the Red Fox of Calenardhon has truly red rather than rust-coloured fur, making it a prized fur target. Fortunately it is also cleverer than the average fox, wily about avoiding Men and traps, and rarely stumbles into death, so it must be actively hunted. A canny thief, it steals hens, rabbits and small bright objects, and unlike most carnivores enjoys some colour vision.

name: Marsh Mastiffs
description: Tall, domesticated dogs found wherever the Dunedain have resided or held extensive commercial links, with long legs and short wiry coats of dark brown or russet. Well-liked by shepherds, herders and hunters, they are sure-footed, good swimmers, keen-nosed, fearless and loyal, and are often fitted with collars and even leather coats.

name: Gích
description: Large fox (pl. "Gích") found throughout the foothills of the Misty Mountains and the chief small predator of Rhudaur. Its name means "changer": black in summer, white in winter, and striped while moulting, a phase the superstitious Rhudaurans hold sacrosanct and believe weapon-proof. Eats small mammals, eggs, ripe fruit and carrion; its fur is marketable but not especially valuable.

name: Dunmen's Dogs
description: Squat dogs resembling a large, fat toy hound, kept only by the tribal Dunlendings. Sometimes used as guards or for pack-hunting, at which they are marginally capable; most Dunmen's Dogs end up in the stewpot.

name: The Majmun
description: Hordes of lively monkeys living among the trees of the forest called "Gaj" in western Far Harad, about three feet head to tail and flitting through branches like squirrels. Less intelligent than their more developed relatives but endearing to Men who visit the Gaj, they are neither noisy nor unclean and act tame around typical Haradan, though shyer with outlandishly dressed folk; great swarms haunt the temple grounds so respectfully the faithful insist they worship there. Their attractive gold-and-black fur is speckled with light that glows in the dark, and tales tell of foreign pelt-trappers who met ghastly ends, swarmed by the chattering beasts.

name: Undarlaif
description: Large black jackals (Rh. Leavings) that entered the Talath Harroch, the southern Rhovanion plain below Mirkwood, during the Great Plague of T.A. 1636-1637, having migrated from the East; they now range from the western flanks of the Orocarni to the eastern bank of the Anduin. Their reddish ears and eerie blue eyes give an innocent guise that hides sly, pitiless ways. Hunting at night in coordinated packs of 4-20, they encircle prey, decoy the strongest defenders and close on the choicest victims, scavenging carrion in lean times.

name: White Foxes
description: Found throughout the temperate highlands and northern plains of Middle-earth, the White Fox is especially famous in Calenardhon (Rohan), where it is rare and prized as a hunting foe by Gondorian frontiersmen and Rohir lords. It is only white in winter; its dense pelt is normally bright silvery grey and, like the Grass Cat, picks up grass pollen to look oddly light green, while black-tipped guard hairs keep even the winter coat from being truly white. It dens in shallow burrows and eats small mammals, ground birds and their eggs, and large insects such as grasshoppers.

name: White Wolves
description: Northerly kin of the Dire Wolves (S. Drauglos, Q. Lossanarmo), distantly related to the common Grey Wolf and true denizens of the cold, thriving at temperatures as low as -60 degrees F. Most at home in the Far North, they have been seen as far south as the Shire in Eriador and the Celduin Valley in Rhovanion during especially bad winters. The harsh fight to survive makes them even cleverer and bolder than Dire Wolves, preying on everything from rodents and carrion to well-armed Lossoth, with a glistening coat of hollow hairs, two-chambered nostrils and furred feet fit for ice and snow.

name: Uncavi
description: Large cat (the Unca) of the hilly woodlands of Far Harad, often the only large mammal in its ecosystem and rather smaller than the Leopard it resembles. Its pelt is splotched in shades of brown for perfect camouflage as it prowls the overgrown jungle floor or hides in low branches; since most local prey is small and quick it spends much time chasing through the brush, though its sturdy build lets it take larger game. It avoids the deadly Petla, hunts the playful Stetan, and will take Men by surprise.

name: Ants
description: Industrious ants whose great earthen nests dot wood and plain; most are harmless, but the biting and soldier kinds swarm in defense, and the largest southern species can strip a carcass with frightening speed.

name: Vuk
description: Wild hill dog of the arid and semi-arid hills of southern Endor, abundant in Far Harad, Chy, Vaag and Gaathgykarkan and ranging east to Horl. It resembles a wolf but with a larger, squarer snout and a mottled dark-tan coat spotted grey and brown for camouflage. With a wolf's strength and a jackal's pride it is resilient, hunting in packs and turning to carrion when game is scarce; rather than ambush it runs prey to exhaustion, circling and giving off yelping barks until even the region's tough wild goats fall.

name: Mûmakil
description: The huge, fearsome, and majestic Mûmak (W. "Oliphaunt"; P. "Tembo"; pl. "Tembovi") is a close relative of the Andamunda (Elephant) and is often referred to as the Gorsandamunda (Q. "Giant Elephant"). It is the "lord" of Endor's southern wilds, growing up to thirty feet in length and standing up to 17 feet in height at the shoulder, with few foes. Fortunately for their rivals, Mûmakil are vegetarians; they rest in the midday heat and roam in search of nutritious roots, leaves, shoots, twigs, and fruits, using their agile trunks and huge tusks (which grow as long as 7'). These social beasts travel in herds and adapt (albeit reluctantly) to domestication. When aroused, they can be deadly fighters; both the Úsakani and the Mûmakani have long prized them as mounts for war, and the Haradrim build great fighting towers to place on their backs, decorating the legs and tusks. The thick hides of Mûmakil make them almost invulnerable to arrows except for their eyes, but when blinded they often run mad with pain, trampling and destroying everything in their paths.

name: Nazgûl
description: The Nazgul or Ringwraiths, true Lords of the Undead and the single most powerful beings of their kind ever to walk Middle-earth; the nine Wraith-kings serve from Mordor (Minas Morgul) and Dol Guldur. (Detailed in ICE's Peoples of Middle-earth series.)

name: Uvag-Aak
description: Blood-drinking apes of the Tanturak jungle of the Mumakan in southernmost Endor, who defeated the Men and Elves that once sought to settle the region. Intensely territorial, they will not leave their jungle unless food becomes scarce; blood is their primary and preferred nutrition, though they eat flesh in lean times. Fiercely protective of their own, they exact horrible vengeance on any who kill one of their kind, leaving the blood-drained corpses of their victims where other would-be exterminators are sure to see them.

name: Vodyanoi
description: Aquatic carnivore and relative of the Giant Hellbender, resembling a giant black frog and preferring the flesh of people. It lurks in dark pools, dragging prey underwater to drown or swallowing it whole by dislocating its jaw like a snake; its huge eyes can hypnotise and distract its victims (5th-level RR).

name: Red Jaws
description: Ugly, scaleless cavern fish especially common in Moria, with eyes set on top of its head and gaping shark-like jaws of up to seven rows of teeth (harmless to Dwarves). Colorless save for a bright red jaw and luminescent fins that flick brilliantly in dark water; it swallows a dog or goat whole or devours a Dwarf's leg in seconds.

name: Demon Whales
description: Huge carnivorous white or grey creatures of the Ice Bay of Forochel, with five-inch steel-tearing teeth that can swallow a man whole (survival in the acidic stomach being unlikely). They often shatter whale-boats or overturn larger vessels.

name: Zûrafy
description: The Zûrafy (V. "Tallnecks"; sing. "Zûrafa") of Núrad, northern Chey, and the Talathrant Valley resemble giraffes with long, spikelike horns. They are related to Deer and feed on the foliage, fruit, and buds found in the upper part of trees or tall scrub; in harsher locales the Zûrafa will stoop to eat grasses, and herds have been known to devastate crops. The Núriags, Variags, and Chey use the Zûrafy as mounts, for they are swift, fearless, and exceedingly fierce combatants. Because of their limited numbers and the difficulty in capturing and domesticating them, only small numbers have been employed outside the circles of royalty. Most Zûrafy have a whitish or light tan coat and brown-and-white striped legs; their horns are covered with short white hair which they never shed. The rare, omnivorous, nocturnal Variag Zûrafa is jet black.

name: Marsh-Drakes
description: Marsh-drakes are small Fire-drakes of the wetlands whose extremely toxic secretions act like diluted Dragon-blood.

name: Colbran
description: Pukel-creatures, the strongest and most clever works of the Druedain and the Daen Coentis: animated stone or metal that stays unseen until activated, then takes the form of attenuated manlike beings glowing with fierce blue light, some casting lightning bolts.

name: Land Drakes
description: Land-drakes are one of the three wingless varieties of Cold-drake (Earth element), keeping to northern highlands and plains.

name: Crebain
description: Large black crows with evil natures, Crebain (sing. "Craban") have often been servants of evil. They are primarily carrion eaters, and they are not picky about how long their meals have been dead or even about whether they are truly dead yet. They will also mob injured creatures and, if under instructions from some evil intelligence, uninjured individuals travelling alone or lost. Keen-sighted and attracted by shining or brightly-colored objects, they make excellent spies. They are most common on south-central Middle-earth, including Fangorn and Dunland, but may be found elsewhere, especially near centers of evil.

name: Barrow Wights
description: Malevolent spirits that haunt the Barrow-downs (Tyrn Gorthad), inhabiting the burial mounds of ancient Men and dressing their victims in grave-treasure.

name: Rain-Drakes
description: Rain-drakes (Q. "Aelinileki") are relatives of the true Water-drakes that dwell in fresh water, spending much time in shallows and building lairs near it.

name: Water-Drakes
description: True Water-drakes, often called Sea Serpents, are agile swimming Dragons (freshwater and saltwater) that seize prey with six fin-claws; their one weakness, besides fire, is being lured from water.

name: Specters
description: Uncommon in Middle-earth, Specters are cold, white, incorporeal figures akin to Ghosts. Victims killed by Specters frequently become Specters themselves. The Balrog of Moria, for instance, had his minions throw captives into caverns and pits known to be haunted by these creatures. The Specters' need for energy is so great that even the lesser kind drain 8 points of constitution per round. They cannot be affected by nonmagical weapons because of their insubstantial nature. The Specters of Moria can cast Shock Bolts, in addition to their energydraining abilities.

name: Winged Cold-Drakes
description: Winged Cold-drakes, like Fire-drakes, descend from the true Cold-drakes; they keep to northern mountain lairs (notably the Grey Mountains) and raid Dwarven settlements.

name: Mewlips
description: hnwmskt-FM,CDHJP,EQRY-8; 6-11 ft Mewlips are an evil, semi-legendary race of exceedingly rare, cannibalistic spirits. These shadowy spirits exist to drink blood, which they need almost as much as other undead need life-energy. Denizens of graveyards, ruins, and other pleasant sites, they are often found surprisingly near inhabited areas, especially if they have not found much sustenance farther afield lately. Mewlips are rare even for Undead. Fond of the most noisome and darkest swamps and marshes, they have a silent tread and strangling hands, although they typically use jagged weapons of rusty steel or stone. They are deceptively human in appearance: however, their backs are horribly hunched and their skin shines with a clammy, greenish-brown palor. Even their ragged clothing is moist and foul. Mewlips feed on most anything and they covet shiny objects, especially things of gold. It is said that such items remind them of uncursed life, of the nature of beingsnot in the thrall of Darkness.

name: Ghouls
description: Ghouls are disgusting animated corpses that have the lengthened teeth and nails considered typical of a one- or two-month old corpse. They are preserved by the conditions around them, either tannin-loaded swampy water or drying winds, and they are animated by some evil power. Stupid and remembering little if any of their past, Ghouls are dominated by thoughts of hunger for energy and defense of their territory. The average Ghoul simply rushes at its victims, tearing with teeth and nails. Their gruesome appearance i n s t i l l s Fear (victims must make a successful RR or flee), while their filthy and rotting bodies create wounds that often (01-30) become infected. the Undead. They are the single most powerful beings of this nature ever to walk Middle-earth. All nine of these Wraithkings are described in detail in ICE's Lords of Middle-earth, Volume II.

name: Corpse Candles
description: Corpse Lanterns, being both stronger and more i n t e l l i g e n t than Corpse Candles, weave a stronger and more alluring web, appearing whole and wholesome above the waters of the marsh or swamp, and seeming to the victim to be something that would attract his attention. The visions vary according to the victim, and several unfortunates gazing upon the same Corpse Lantern may see different things: the lusty warrior may see a buxom wench with a lantern, searching for something; the compassionate Healer a lost child with a lamp; the suspicious thief a fat merchant with a purse that could use lightening, engaged in burying more treasure in the safe embrace of the marsh. Thus the Corpse Lantern entices its victims to a death by drowning, enabling the undead remnant to drain 5 Co points per round in the interim.

name: Fiara
description: Thriving on the scant grasses of south-central Eriador, the Fiara is the most common wild herbivore in Cardolan and Minhiriath. They keep to small groups of four or five does and a buck and range all over the region. Extremely timid, though, they shy away from settled areas and seem much rarer than their real numbers would dictate. It is fortunate that these small beasts are prolific, for no creature in northwestern Endor is hunted as often as the Fiara. Eriadoran hunters have long relied on the Fiara as a food staple, and cooks from Bree to Tharbad create dozens of dishes using this tasty Deer meat.

name: Werewolves
description: Huge, intelligent enchanted wolves first made by Morgoth, able to stand and use clawlike hands, speak, use tools and cast spells but never become Men. Caran-Carach (Red Fang) guarded Dol Guldur, home of the Necromancer, and hunted the Wood-elves and Wood-men of southern Mirkwood.

name: Vessino Clams
description: The Vessino Clam is a rare mollusk which is found only in the deep waters of the Haragaer and the southern Belegaer. A source of a valuable ingredient for a healing balm that soothes and heals burns, it is prized by Lay Healers and Medicinists. Its beautiful, silvery shell is also noted as an exquisite ornament.

name: Spotted Lions
description: Named for the black spots on its almost-white underbelly above a burnt-orange coat, the Spotted Lion is now quite rare from hunting but still ranges throughout Cardolan. Not evil but extremely cunning and speechless, its old, bitter feud with the wolves has hastened its decline alongside the efforts of Men. It lives in small family groups, lairs in trees and caves, prefers to hunt Fiara (sheep will do), and will kill men in self-defence.

name: Sea Crocodiles
description: Uncommon but well-feared, the huge Sea Crocodiles resemble Fell Beasts without wings. They are gracile, dark blue, and have black stripes running down the backs of their horny backs. Many chroniclers believe they are related to Water Drakes, for Sea Crocodiles have elongated conical grasping teeth in the forward part of their snouts that extend well outside their mouths. Like sea monsters, Sea Crocodiles are prone to attack smaller ships, seeking to puncture hulls with their mighty jaws, then waiting for the ship to founder. Nesting in secluded bays or in coastal caves, they also present a danger on land.

name: Lesinavi
description: Ghoul-like undead that prowl the southern wastes, lairing in the caves of dry hills or among the dunes over unmarked graves; their dried eyes perceive the life-energy of victims as painful light, and they ignore stun and pain.

name: Devevi
description: The Devevi (Ap. "Camels"; sing. "Deve") of southern and central Endor are hardy animals who store water in their stomach linings and can go long periods without drinking. Their humps absorb heat and metabolize fat to provide energy and water, while wide foot pads enable them to walk on soft, sandy soil; a three-chambered stomach permits digestion of virtually any vegetation. With a refined temperature regulation system, a Deve has no need to sweat; it can lose over a quarter of its body weight and restore it in an orgy of drinking lasting only ten minutes (intaking 7-8 gallons a minute). There are two subgroups: the runners (light, graceful riding mounts built for speed) and the workers (thickly-built, slow, capable of heavy loads). Both are easy to domesticate but are loud, filthy, spit, and are virtually impossible to keep clean. The most prized is the rare two-humped Deve Póa, bred in and around the desert city of Tûl Póac in Far Harad and hailing from the Yellow Mountains of southern Endor; adapted for both temperature extremes, more docile, with a natural saddle and a smooth, rolling gait.

name: Cherethrynd
description: Powerful Sperm Whales, the Cherethrynd (S. "Hallmaw"; sing. "Charothrond") reside in the deep waters of the Belegaer, off the western coasts of Middle-earth. Unlike most Whales, they sport teeth (nine inch long teeth) and they hunt fish and Squid rather than consuming krill through a baleen strainer. The Charothrond favorite food and most dangerous rival is the Giant Squid. Although wily and capable of upsetting a good-sized vessel, Cherethrynd are vulnerable to the ruthless Umbarean whalers who sail the oceans near the Bay of Belfalas. These Men hunt lone Whales for their oil, bone, and ambergris (an indigestible chalky substance that forms around Squid beaks and, in spite of its hideous smell, makes wonderful perfumes).

name: Beacon
description: A signal-beacon set on hill or tower, lit in time of war or need so that warning may leap from height to height across the land. A landmark by day and a summons by night.

name: Cave Drakes
description: Cave-drakes (Q. "Rondoleki") are relatively small, ancient wingless Dragons that rely on instinct rather than plots, hunting near their high-mountain cavern lairs with short powerful legs and muscular bodies.

name: Andodailion
description: The Andodaio, or "Gate-shadow" (pl. "Andodailion"), is a black, nocturnal lizard with a prehensile tongue and tail. It is without doubt the greatest climber among all the Rávatsar. Rugged landscapes and subterranean settings accommodate it well, for the Andodaio is insulated with an unusual array of three layers of hollow scales that enable it to survive in environments far too cool for its brethren. Hanging from rocks, it swings across the caves and precipices, leaping onto prey and tearing its victims apart with its steely claws. Larger creatures would have little to fear from the Andodailion, except that they live in large communities and often hunt in groups of 10-100 individuals. Unusually social, a horde of Andodailion can easily fell a Man by attacking in concert.

name: Glutani
description: Dreadful wolverine-like predator (pl. Glutani), solitary, dark-furred and badger-shaped, found throughout northwestern Endor with its greatest concentration in western Minhiriath. Unbelievably quick, fierce and seemingly insane, it attacks armed groups without provocation and fights to the death. Two varieties: the reddish-brown Lowland Glutan (~2 ft) and the bulkier, dark Highland Glutan of the Dunland hills. A 25 gp bounty sits on their heads; Dunlanders buy live ones for fights against Bears or Wolves.

name: Ghosts
description: These can be the most powerful of undead, save the Nazgûl (see below). They are totally energy, having no physical component at all, and are activated solely by the presence of living beings of the same type that they once were (most often Men). They appear as they remember themselves being, with varying degrees of effectiveness: lesser Ghosts often neglect pertinent details, such as appropriate clothing for the occasion. They also do not realize that, since their appearence is nothing but illusion, their will can affect it. A Ghost who wishes to be larger and more menacing is so, with nothing but a thought. Greater Ghosts, whose instincts are under more control, avoid this giveaway and behave in a very lifelike manner, enabling them to drain the constitution (and hence l i t e energy) of a victim while engaging him in conversation or merely being near him. It is a strange fact that most Ghosts seem not to realize that they are dead; often, if they can be convinced of their true nature, they will dissipate and go forth to their appointed rest. (Note that Elves do not have Ghosts, since their spirits always know where to go. It is notable that Elves do not fear Ghosts, perhaps for this reason). However, Ghosts are remarkably impervious to reason on this score (How would you. Reader, react if someone insisted that you were dead?), and would-be persuaders are well-warned that there is no way that the Ghost can avoid draining their life-energy, any more than they themselves can avoid having their hearts beat. Anyone near enough to a Ghost to speak to it is near enough to be drained. Some Ghosts are bound to a place or object that was very important to them in life or that figured strongly in their deaths: a sentry killed while guarding a crucial post, for instance, whose spirit remains "on duty" long after the fortress that he guarded has fallen to ruins; the murder victim haunting the gold that she was killed for; the child plague victim still holding fast to the wooden doll that comforted its last hours.

name: Wild Horses
description: The Wild Horse, or Horse of the Plains (S. "Rechelaid"), make good mounts, although they are hard to break. Much used by Easterlings, they are now herded in Calenardhon (Rohan). A few may be found in the marshes, for they do not mind swimming.

name: Sloth Bear
description: Dark-brown Sloth Bear (Q. Meglituika), native to northwestern Harad and especially prevalent around Umbar, the largest bear in Middle-earth at 2,500-4,000 lbs. A huge, slow, stupid beast like a cross between a bear and a rainforest sloth, it uses formidable claws to dig vast tunnel networks, even through crumbling rock, large enough for Men to live in. Little-fearing and hard to kill thanks to its bulk and unusually thick skull, it eats whatever it can claw in the mass it needs to sustain itself.

name: Grey Apes
description: Grey, shaggy-haired, heavily-built apes (P. "Cijuvu Niavi", S. "Peredrugwath") that live alone, in pairs or in small family groups in the forests of Pel in southwestern Middle-earth. They stand up to 5 ft tall and weigh up to 300 lbs; long arms reach the ankles when erect, and broad opposable-digited hands plus great upper-body strength let them swing tree to tree with ease. Their short, weak legs make for an awkward, halting gait on the jungle floor, whether upright or on all fours.

name: Ash Drake
description: An ash/volcanic variety of Fire-drake, at home amid cinder and lava fields.

name: Arthedain Site
description: A ruin or relic of Arthedain, northern kingdom of the Dúnedain — a fallen watchtower, road-marker or hall crumbling on the downs of old Arnor. Some still hold lore, treasure or older guardians.

name: Thrushes
description: Thrushes are large songbirds. Nearly as intelligent and gifted as ravens, they are among Radagast the Brown's principal messengers and scouts. They are also long-time friends of wise Men in many regions, but especially in Erebor and the Dale. Dwarves admire them, but cannot communicate with them because the Thrushes speak too quickly and in too high pitched voices (one would imagine that Ents would have similar problems). The Thrush language can, however, be learned by Men and Elves who care enough to make a study of such things. Thrushes are very keen eyed and have a strong streak of curiosity in them, so they make excellent scouts. The Old Thrush of Erebor helped Thorin and Company find the secret entrance to Smaug's cave, and warned Bard the Bowman of Smaug's weak spot.

name: Coireals
description: This inoffensive snake inhabits forests and heavy copses; it can be readily identified by the bright yellow bands on its glossy black body. It is generally docile and will avoid people. Most bites occur when witless folk try to pick up this "pretty snake." Instead, the Coireal is happy to hunt large insects. It should be left alone, for its venom is highly potent (10th lvl), one of the most lethal natural poisons in Middle-earth. Symptoms appear in one to four hours, resulting in all but certain death from paralysis.

name: Land Tortoises
description: Land Tortoises were once common in southern Eriador and western Gondor, particularly in the Gwathló basin. Unfortunately, they are extremely tasty and nutritious, so they are favored by Eriadoran hunters and are nearly extinct. Their beautiful green and grey shells are now exceptionally rare. Prized for their value as armor (the shell makes excellent reinforcement) and (when ground) as a source of medicine to combat blood diseases and ailments, a Land Tortoise shell can bring up to two hundred gold pieces on the open market. Very slow and inoffensive, the Land Tortoise's primary defense is its size: the average specimen is five feet long and three high, weighing about three hundred pounds; a few older ones reach six feet and over a quarter ton. When attacked they retreat until cornered, then ground themselves and pull into their shells, or lash out with their necks and hard, sharp beaks. They feed on nearly every type of vegetation and roam the highlands for richer grass or shrubs. Females dig holes along river banks or on the seashore, lay 11-20 eggs, cover them with moist soil, and abandon them.

name: Ulmodili
description: No water creature is as intelligent or blessed as the Ulmodili (Q. "Friend of Ulmo"; pl. "Ulmodili"), the beautiful blue and white Dolphin (Porpoise). Seaborne mammals, they are servants of Ulmo (as opposed to Ossë) and fast allies of the Teleri. Their complex society and wide-ranging schools enable them to act with startling unity when summoned by their master or friends, or when aroused by their enemies. The gregarious Ulmodili live in hierarchical groups of 10-200 individuals, which in turn join to form schools of 100-2000 creatures. Playful and agile, they roll and leap along the surface of the open sea, moving at speeds of up to 40 miles an hour. When they submerge, they can dive as deep as 1000 feet and stay down for as long as five minutes before they need to resurface (they breathe through lungs, not gills). An Ulmodili has smooth blue (top) and white (bottom) skin and a long beaked snout. This snout is a formidable, mace-like weapon that contains an array of short, sharp teeth. Its bulging forehead encases a pad of fat that houses a sonar-like organ that enables the creature to detect objects with stunning accuracy.

name: Great Falcons Of Ardor
description: These fierce creatures were the preferred mounts of the Lords of the Air of the Court of Ardor in southern Middle-earth. They were perhaps developed from the Great Falcons of Mirkwood. This fact would have been known to Sauron, who could have shared his knowledge with the evil Elda Court. The fiercest of these Falcons were tied to specific Windlords by magical means; their loyalty to their masters was unswerving, and no one else could ride them unless they could somehow overcome the will of the Falcons by magical means.

name: Water Spring
description: A spring where water rises from the ground — a welcome find for the thirsty, though some run foul, mineral-tainted or scalding, and a few are held sacred or guarded by what dwells near them.

name: Boars
description: Fierce wild pigs, ancestors of all domesticated breeds, found throughout the temperate regions of Endor; the largest, the Red Boar, lives east of the Orocarni in the easternmost continent (particularly Aegan). Exceptionally dangerous and largely nocturnal, an alarmed Boar charges regardless of size, trampling with hooves and slashing with tusks, and injuries short of a limb hardly slow it. Hunted everywhere with stout cross-barred spears, since a speared Boar runs up the shaft at the hunter. Omnivores travelling in groups of 1-20; the famed Boar of Everholt of the Firien Wood gored King Folca of Rohan to death in T.A. 2864, boar and king dying together.

name: Sand Devils
description: Tortured spirits of Men who died in desert sandstorms, lingering as whirlwind-like beings that roam the open wastes searching for victims, whom they envelop within their swirling form.

name: Shaking Asps
description: This small, gray snake has a distinctive ruff of loose scales that give off a faint, odd rattling sound when the serpent is startled or about to strike. Its potent venom (level 8) causes massive internal hemorrhage, causing tremendous (24-60 hits) shock and often (with 26+ RR failure) death. The Shaking Asp is a native of Rhudaur, the region in eastern Eriador between the Mitheithel (Hoarwell) and Bruinen (Loudwater) Rivers.

name: Petlavi
description: The Petla (pl. "Petlavi"), or "Forest-strangler," of southwestern Endor is commonly found in the scattered woodlands of Far Harad (particularly in the Forest of Tears). It haunts the oases between Umbar and Drel, lurking in the branches and pools that the Haradrim prize so dearly. One of Middle-earth's most fearsome reptiles, the Petla is an able hunter who has perfected the arts of camouflage and ambush. It is mottled brown in color and blends perfectly with woodland hues. Dropping from above or entangling its prey in water, the snake attacks by using its powerful jaws to gain a grip and then coils its length around its hapless victim. Petlavi reach lengths exceeding thirty feet (one specimen measured forty-five), are over a foot wide at their thickest, and have been known to swallow a whole goat. Their principal weapon is their coiling, constricting body: after a grip is taken (typically after a "B" critical), the body coils around the target over one to ten rounds; it is extremely hard (-30) to break free, and the victim perishes by suffocation in three to thirty minutes. Defenseless against groups, the snake will not often stand and fight when confronted by more than one enemy.

name: Golodos
description: A Golodo is a bright green, moderately large, flightless bird, rather like a large goose on stilts. Excellent swimmers, they subsist primarily on fish, crustaceans, and shellfish. Golodos gather in large flocks during the breeding season in early summer, traveling in small groups during the rest of the year. Golodos will immediately flee the scent of any predator with surprising, if ungainly speed, except during the nesting season, when they will bravely defend their eggs. Given to snapping up carrion, Golodos enjoy the taste of meat; however, they will eat anything, since they are poor hunters.

name: North Bears
description: Huge, pale-grey or white bears (Q. Megliformen) found around the Ice Bay of Forochel. Usually solitary, with an excellent sense of smell and superb swimming, they subsist mainly on seal and fish and range across well-defined coastal territories of about a hundred square miles, smelling intruders up to two miles off. In the brief northern summer they wander beyond their range to find a mate and gorge on roots and berries.

name: Black Minks
description: Voracious freshwater predator, much larger than common mink (50-80 lbs). Most common in the Wetwang (Nindalf) near Ithilien, otherwise in north-central Middle-earth always near water. Hunts alone but mobs in early-spring mating gangs of 7-12, attacking anything in its path. Its thick, glossy, waterproof pelt fetches 6-15 gp.

name: Harfoots (Common Hobbits)
description: The most common Hobbits are Harfoots. They are the smallest in size and the darkest in color, rarely exceeding 3 feet in height and having brown skin and hair. Neat and uniformly beardless, they are in many ways the picture of the average Hobbit. Harfoots favor hillsides and highlands. They have traditional sites for their traditional smials or Hobbit-hole houses. They prefer to interact with the grim Dwarves. All Hobbits share in hating Wargs and Orcs. They are the stay-at-home, hole-dwelling folk of the Shire, Bree and the old Hobbit-lands of Eriador, and are seldom met far afield.

name: Stoors (River Hobbits)
description: The Stoors are the stockiest Hobbit folk and often appear shorter than some of the Harfoots because of their wide profile. Generally, however, they are taller than Harfoots and shorter than Fallohides. Their coloration is also somewhat of a compromise, although they have curly brown hair which resembles that of their shorter cousins. Nonetheless, the most distinguishing features of Stoors are their huge hands and feet (large even by Hobbit standards) and frequent beard growth. Stoors prefer riversides and flatlands. Some have begun relying on surface dwellings of wood, brick, or stone. Stoors trace their lines through the female. River- and marsh-loving folk, they are met poling boats and tending weirs in Dunland, the wetlands of Eriador and the Gladden country, and are warier of strangers than other Hobbits.
